summary: Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting for regular business, featuring a comprehensive 2025 budget presentation by Administrator Rose covering all township departments. The board discussed HSA contribution increases for employees, approved several personnel actions, and celebrated recent successes including the passage of a fire levy and the Station 18 groundbreaking. An executive session was held to discuss employee compensation, after which the board approved 2025 non-union wages, a multi-year employment agreement for Bob Rose, and a one-time ARPA-funded bonus for department heads.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda - passed
Motion to approve payment of bills from October 22 through November 14, 2024 - passed
Motion to increase HSA employer contributions to $1,500 single and $2,500 family effective first pay date January 2025, moving from 3 categories to 2 - passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Chief Myers to attend FDIC in Indianapolis April 8-12, 2025 - passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Matthew Lieber effective November 19, 2024 - passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter paramedic Keaton Hall at $17.50/hour effective November 17, 2024 - passed
Motion to authorize Administrative Assistant Evan Corddry to assist zoning inspector - passed
Motion to approve township building duct cleaning by Stanley Steamer at $3,750 - passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:15 p.m. - passed
Motion to approve 2025 non-union wages - passed
Motion to adjust HR Director Melinda Eichenhorst's annual vacation accrual to 120 hours effective January 1, 2025 - passed
Motion to approve employment agreement with Bob Rose as Police Chief/Administrator/Public Safety Director for 2025-2029 - passed
Motion to approve use of remaining ARPA funds for one-time bonus to department heads - passed
Motion to adjourn at 8:23 p.m. - passed
action_items: Administrator Bob Rose to contact Matthew Hammond regarding his proposed winery/wedding venue project at 6550 Smith Road and relay individual trustee feedback
Department heads to review budgets and look for areas to trim to reduce the projected $300,000 deficit
Administrator Rose to prepare a breakout of capital expenditures separate from operating budgets for future budget discussions
Chad Meadows to coordinate putting up decorations before the Light Up Goshen Parade on November 23
Chief Myers to follow up with Clermont County Building Department on Station 18 building permits
Laura to submit lighting district evaluation to auditor annually every August going forward
Administrator Rose to provide detailed breakdown of contracted services line item in general fund

attendees: Administrator Bob Rose (also referred to as Chief Rose)
notes: Meeting was a special meeting for regular business held at 6 p.m. The chair's name appears inconsistently in the transcript as both Halterman and Heiserman. The trustees noted they cannot legally meet together outside formal settings (Sunshine Law), directing the public speaker to work through the administrator. Chief Myers announced his engagement during the meeting. The board expressed concern about a projected ~$300,000 deficit in the 2025 budget, primarily driven by capital expenditures. ARPA funds will be fully exhausted by end of 2024. Executive session ran from approximately 7:15 p.m. to 8:21 p.m. The transcript references Clermont County but also mentions 'Fairmont County Building Department' which appears to be a transcription error for Clermont County.
title: BOT 11-14-24
